title: Celebrating 20 Years of the Wikimedia Affiliations Committee
content:
On 15 January 2006, the Wikimedia Affiliations Committee (AffCom) was established, coinciding with the fifth anniversary of Wikipedia. Over the past two decades, AffCom has played a central role in supporting the growth and development of Wikimedia affiliates, helping communities around the world organize, collaborate, and contribute to the Wikimedia movement.

What began as a Chapters Committee focused on coordinating the development and recognition of Wikimedia Chapters has evolved alongside the movement itself. As new forms of affiliate organizations emerged, AffCom expanded its mandate to better reflect the growing diversity of Wikimedia communities and their needs.

Throughout the two decades of its activities, AffCom has been shaped by several important milestones:

  • 2012 – AffCom’s responsibilities expanded to include the recognition and support of Wikimedia User Groups and Thematic Organizations.
  • 2012 – The committee’s first Charter was approved by the Wikimedia Foundation Board of Trustees, providing a formal framework for its work. It also changed the name from Chapters Committee to Affiliations Committee.
snippet: On 15 January 2006, the Wikimedia Affiliations Committee (AffCom) was established, coinciding with the fifth anniversary of Wikipedia. Over the past two decades, AffCom has played a central role in
creator: Մարի Ավետիսյան

title: Hindi Wiki Sammelan 2026: A Decade of Building Community, Shaping Knowledge, and Preparing for the Future
content:
Dr. Syed Muzammiluddin

As the Hindi Wiki Sammelan or Conference returns in 2026, it offers an opportunity not only to discuss the future of free knowledge in Hindi but also to reflect on a remarkable journey that began more than a decade ago. From the first gathering of Hindi Wikipedians in Delhi in 2015 to subsequent conferences in Bhopal, Delhi, Kolkata, and Delhi again, the Hindi Wiki Sammelans have helped transform a geographically dispersed group of volunteers into a vibrant and collaborative community.

The story began in 2015 when Hindi Wikipedians came together in Delhi to explore how regular face-to-face interaction could strengthen the community. Participants discussed outreach, partnerships, editor recruitment, and ways to increase public awareness about Hindi Wikipedia. More importantly, the meetup established a shared vision for the future.
creator: Hindustanilanguage

title: Understand the forces shaping free knowledge: Wikimania 2026 public policy advocacy sessions to join
content:
Wikimania is extra special this year. The Wikimedia movement celebrates an extraordinary milestone: Wikipedia’s 25th birthday. Anniversaries are an opportunity to look ahead. As the internet undergoes profound transformation, an important question emerges for this Wikimania: How do we ensure that free knowledge projects and the communities behind them can continue to thrive for the next 25 years?

Global trends in technology and policy are radically changing the context in which the open knowledge movement operates. It is clear that the next 25 years will require us to adapt. Governments are introducing new rules on online safety, age verification, artificial intelligence (AI), platform accountability, and digital sovereignty. At the same time, generative AI is changing how knowledge is created, discovered, and reused, raising new questions about the sustainability of the commons, the value of human contribution, and the future of trustworthy information online.
snippet: Wikimania is extra special this year. The Wikimedia movement celebrates an extraordinary milestone: Wikipedia’s 25th birthday. Anniversaries are an opportunity to look ahead. As the internet undergoes
creator: Franziska Putz

title: “Hidden Caribbean Figures”: an editing event to make Caribbean Literature visible on Wikipedia
content:
In  March 2026, I had the opportunity to participate as a co-facilitator in the event “Hidden Figures of the Caribbean: An Editing Event to Visualize Caribbean Literature.” It was a two-day Wikipedia editing workshop at Mona Campus of the University of the West Indies (UWI), in Kingston, Jamaica focused on creating and improving articles, with the goal of reducing the content gap about the Caribbean in the online encyclopedia.

This event was  a collaboration between the Create Caribbean Research Institute (CCRI) founded and led by Dr. Schuyler Esprit and recognized as the first digital humanities center in the Caribbean; Whose Knowledge?, a global feminist collective to center the knowledge of marginalized communities (the majority of the world) on the Internet, including Wikimedia projects; and Noircir Wikipedia, an initiative that aims to address the shortcomings in terms of references, articles and information on African and Afro-descendant diaspora culture and personalities on Wikipedia, whose work focuses on addressing biases stemming from colonial history, such as the euphemism for historical crimes, the use of racist stereotypes or a Eurocentric selection of sources.
snippet: In March 2026, I had the opportunity to participate as a co-facilitator in the event “Hidden Figures of the Caribbean: An Editing Event to Visualize Caribbean Literature.” It was a two-day Wikipedia
creator: Mariana Fossatti WK

title: Water for Life, Knowledge for All: How WikiVerse Botswana is Showcasing Africa’s Water Story Through the Africa Wiki Challenge 2026
content:
Every day, millions of Africans depend on rivers, lakes, wetlands, groundwater, and sanitation systems that sustain communities, economies, and ecosystems. Yet many of these stories remain underrepresented on the internet, limiting access to reliable, locally relevant knowledge about one of the continent’s most vital resources.

To help bridge this knowledge gap, WikiVerse Botswana organized the Africa Wiki Challenge 2026, bringing together volunteers, students, and emerging Wikimedia contributors to document Africa’s water heritage under the theme “Water for Life in Africa: Documenting the State of Africa’s Water Resources and Sanitation Systems.” Inspired by the African Union’s 2026 theme on sustainable water availability and safe sanitation, the campaign encouraged participants to improve free knowledge while contributing to global conversations on sustainable development.
snippet: Every day, millions of Africans depend on rivers, lakes, wetlands, groundwater, and sanitation systems that sustain communities, economies, and ecosystems. Yet many of these stories remain
creator: Mothusi Sekhomba

title: Celebrating Wikipedia’s 25th Anniversary in Kyrgyzstan
content:
In 2001, what started as a small online encyclopedia has grown into a global movement for free knowledge. Over the past 25 years, Wikipedia and its surrounding ecosystem have been built and expanded through the collective efforts of millions of volunteers around the world.

In Kyrgyzstan, the Wikimedia community continues to grow through the dedication of local contributors who create and improve free knowledge in the Kyrgyz language and beyond. To celebrate Wikipedia’s 25th anniversary, members of the Kyrgyz Wikimedia community organized a special gathering.

The celebration took place on May 28, 2026, and was fully organized by Wikimedian Incall, who coordinated the preparations and ensured that everything was ready for the event.

An invitation was shared with the local Wikimedia community, and around 15 people registered to participate. On the day of the celebration, eight participants attended. Although the gathering was relatively small, it provided an excellent opportunity for everyone to meet in person, exchange ideas, and spend time together in a warm and friendly atmosphere.
snippet: In 2001, what started as a small online encyclopedia has grown into a global movement for free knowledge. Over the past 25 years, Wikipedia and its surrounding ecosystem have been built and expanded
creator: Incall

title: Obinna Tony: Making Queer Histories Visible in Nigeria
content: Last month, we had the opportunity to speak with Obinna Tony-Francis Ochem (Lagos, Nigeria, 1996, pronouns: he/they). Obinna, who lives in Lagos, is a Nigerian writer, human rights researcher, reporter, journalist, and LGBTQIA+  activist with a degree in Marine Sciences. He is currently the community manager for Wiki for Human Rights, Nigeria, a committee member of the Nigeria National Funding Program (Rapid Fund), and a core organizer for Queering Wiki 2026. Obinna first became involved in theWikimedia universe after attending a “Wiki Loves Pride” edit-a-thon in Lagos, in July 2025. While his involvement with the Wikimedia movement is relatively recent, Obinna more than makes up for it with his dedication and enthusiasm for the cause. After his first edit-a-thon, he began participating in global LGBTQIA+ Wiki meetings and groups. In 2025, he spoke at the Queering Wikipedia online conference. In Nigeria, he organizes virtual training on Wikidata for young queer editors and has led campaigns such as the Trans Visibility Challenge (2025).

creator: Mwvh

title: From Incubator to Approval: How Mentorship Helped the Runyankore Wikipedia Community Grow
content:
What does it take to build a Wikipedia in a language whose contributors often edit from mobile phones, while working through an interface that many find difficult to navigate? For the Runyankore Wikipedia community, the answer has been persistence, practical mentorship, stronger leadership, and a growing network of people and institutions willing to support the work.

At the time of publication, the Language Committee has approved the creation of Runyankore Wikipedia. The project is now awaiting the remaining technical work needed to create its independent site. That approval is a major achievement, but it is not the whole story. Behind it are volunteers who continued contributing despite technical and financial barriers, a community leader learning how to share responsibility, a mentor who helped turn broad ambitions into practical steps, and university students discovering that they could contribute to knowledge in their own language.

This is the story of what happened when the Runyankore community and the Wikimedia Language Diversity Hub worked together.
snippet: What does it take to build a Wikipedia in a language whose contributors often edit from mobile phones, while working through an interface that many find difficult to navigate? For the Runyankore
creator: Owobusingye

title: Data, Dignity, and Difference: Centering Feminist Voices on Wikimedia Projects
content:
When knowledge about women is missing, incomplete, or difficult to find, we not just lose more than information; we lose histories, perspectives, and the opportunity to build a more representative and inclusive record of our world.

Across Wikimedia projects, significant gender gaps continue to shape what knowledge is available online. Although countless women have influenced politics, education, science, social justice, literature, and community development, many of their stories remain absent or underdeveloped across Wikipedia and Wikidata. This challenge is particularly evidenced through the African and non-African feminist leaders, organizations, movements, and initiatives whose contributions are often overlooked or insufficiently documented. Addressing these gaps requires more than creating new articles; it involves ensuring that the existing one are equitably represented across other languages; it calls for building communities that understand the importance of equitable knowledge production and are equipped with the skills to contribute meaningfully.
snippet: When knowledge about women is missing, incomplete, or difficult to find, we not just lose more than information; we lose histories, perspectives, and the opportunity to build a more representative and
creator: Iwuala Lucy

title: Mentor Me! 2026 (First Stream)
content:
The Mentor Me! 2026 program returned this year to support both new and existing Wikimedians in building practical skills across Wikimedia projects like Wikipedia, Wikidata, and Wikimedia Commons. Organized by the Tyap Wikimedians User Group, the program brought together over 70 registered participants for seven weeks of learning, mentorship, and collaboration. The Stream 1 sessions were held online on Fridays via Jitsi Meet and in-person on Saturdays at AX1, Aliyu Makama Road, Barnawa, Kaduna, Nigeria, making it possible for participants to learn regardless of their location.

Through hands-on training led by experienced Wikimedia contributors from within and outside Nigeria, and former mentees who participated in the 2025 edition, participants explored topics ranging from citation and referencing, Wikidata editing, and language documentation to templates, the OpenRefine tool, and other Wikimedia tools, while gaining the confidence to make meaningful contributions to the Wikimedia Movement.
snippet: The Mentor Me! 2026 program returned this year to support both new and existing Wikimedians in building practical skills across Wikimedia projects like Wikipedia, Wikidata, and Wikimedia Commons.
creator: Zasan Doshiyak

title: Reflections from Around the Puzzle Globe
content:
This blog is being translated on Meta-Wiki.

Dear Wikimedians,

I joined the Wikimedia Foundation six months ago, at the beginning of Wikipedia’s 25th birthday. It’s been an extraordinary introduction to this movement.

I have met with more than 1,100 Wikimedians through one-on-one conversations and messages with volunteers and affiliates; Around the Puzzle Globe calls; community events like Futures Lab, WikiCredCon, and the ESEAP conference; and visits to communities in Tokyo, Lisbon, Cape Town, and Johannesburg. Valuable perspectives came from contributors of all kinds—those who are well known among other Wikimedians, those who quietly build Wikimedia projects every day, and even everyday readers of Wikipedia.

What struck me most is the depth of this movement’s commitment to free knowledge. Whether writing and editing articles, mentoring newcomers, building tools, organizing events, translating content, creating partnerships, or serving through affiliates, Wikimedians contribute in countless ways, and every contribution matters.

Across these conversations, one theme emerged consistently: we must adapt to remain relevant in this period of profound change for the internet and Wikimedia.
snippet:
This blog is being translated on Meta-Wiki.

Dear Wikimedians,
creator: Eureka Endo

title: Documenting the Heritage of Indigenous Peoples: A Training Session by Muj(lh)eres Latinoamericanas en Wikimedia
content:
Imagine a world in which every single human being can freely share in the sum of all knowledge. That’s our commitment.

One of the greatest strengths of the Wikimedia movement is that anyone can contribute to making knowledge about their community, culture, and history accessible to the entire world. This was the goal of a training workshop organized by the Escuela de lideresas (School of Women Leaders), a program of Muj(lh)eres Latinoamericanas en Wikimedia (MLA), in which women participants learned how to edit Wikipedia and Wikimedia Commons while documenting a cultural event of great significance to Bolivia.
snippet: Imagine a world in which every single human being can freely share in the sum of all knowledge. That’s our commitment.
creator: Jaluj

title: How to strengthen the public domain with Wikidata
content:
Since 2025, a group of initiatives from Latin America that share a common vision on how to strengthen the cultural heritage of our territories have come together to launch the regional project “Strengthening the Latin American public domain with Wikidata”. In this post we tell you why Wikidata is an excellent tool for improving information on heritage in countries of the Global South, what strategies we consider successful, and how to replicate the initiative in other places.

Access to cultural heritage works in Latin America faces severe limitations due to the scarcity, dispersion, and lack of systematization of data on authors and their copyright status. The lack of open and accessible data hinders digitization projects and disproportionately affects the visibility of marginalized cultural expressions (those of women, diverse groups, and racialized communities).

Added to this are the lengthy copyright terms and the legislative complexities that vary from country to country. Not knowing the legal status of works generates fear of infringing rights, which inhibits the free use of materials that are already in the public domain.
snippet: Since 2025, a group of initiatives from Latin America that share a common vision on how to strengthen the cultural heritage of our territories have come together to launch the regional project
creator: Jorge Gemetto

title: From Local Communities to a Global Movement: Lessons from the Wikimedia Usergroup Nigeria Leaders Retreat 2026
content:
As the Director of Fan Clubs and Networks for Wikimedia Usergroup Nigeria, I recently had the privilege of organising the Wikimedia Nigeria Community Leaders Retreat 2026, a gathering that brought together some of the most passionate leaders within Nigeria’s Wikimedia movement.

From February 2 to 4, 2026 we had the Wikimedia Usergroup Nigeria Staff retreat and from February 4 to 8, the staff was able to join all our community leaders from across the nation to plan for the year. The retreat created a dedicated space for reflection, collaboration, and strategic planning among community leaders who are working every day to expand access to free knowledge through platforms such as Wikipedia and other projects supported by the Wikimedia Foundation.

For many of us, it was also a rare opportunity to pause from our daily community activities and reconnect with the broader purpose of the Wikimedia movement.

The retreat was two phased, the first two days, we had our staff retreat where staff met to discuss our challenges and how we want to improve, then we moved on to hosting our community leaders (Fan Clubs and Network leaders). Our leaders came from all corners of Nigeria.

creator: Kayusyussuf

title: Ghana at Wikimania 2026: Sessions You Don’t Want to Miss
content:
Wikimania, the Wikimedia movement’s biggest annual gathering, returns from 21–25 July 2026 in Paris, France, with the theme “Freedom, Equity, Reliability” (Liberté, Équité, Fiabilité). 

Ghanaian Wikimedians are bringing their ideas, experiences, and innovations to Wikimania 2026, from advancing indigenous language technologies to rethinking sustainable approaches to volunteer support.

Whether you’re attending in Paris or joining online from the comfort of your home, explore this curated list of Ghanaian-led sessions you won’t want to miss, brought to you by the Wikimedia Ghana User Group.

If you’re wondering which sessions to add to your Wikimania 2026 schedule, start here! Whether you’re passionate about language preservation, volunteer support, or emerging Wikimedia technologies, there’s something here for you.

  • Date: Friday, 24 July 2026 
  • Time: 2:30 PM – 3:25 PM (Europe/Paris) 
  • Speaker(s): Mohammed Kamal-Deen Fuseini (Dnshitobu)

    • Why attend?

    Discover how Wikifunctions can support indigenous languages and learn practical ways to prepare language data needed to participate fully in emerging multilingual knowledge systems. 

    🔗Session: wikimedia.eventyay.com/wm/wikimania2026/talk/TWGKWB/
snippet: Wikimania, the Wikimedia movement’s biggest annual gathering, returns from 21–25 July 2026 in Paris, France, with the theme “Freedom, Equity, Reliability” (Liberté, Équité, Fiabilité).
creator: Pnelson WMGH
